---
title: "Dwarf Live Oak (Quercus minima)"
slug: "quercus-minima"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622168426/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Dwarf Live Oak"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Fabales"
tax_family: "Fagaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Fabales", "Fagaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622168426/medium.jpg" alt="Dwarf Live Oak" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/tylaughlin">
tylaughlin</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341730849">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Fabales
-   **Family:** Fagaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=167658)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in dry, sandy habitats such as scrub and sandhills.

## Natural history notes

A low-growing, shrubby oak characterized by its small, thick, evergreen leaves and sprawling growth form that often stays close to the ground. In Florida, it is found in dry, sandy habitats such as scrub and sandhills, where it spreads by underground stems. It produces acorns that provide food for wildlife and is well adapted to fire-maintained ecosystems.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/167658-Quercus-minima)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/2589)
